reasoning: The informal statement uses $\{A_\alpha\}$ to denote a collection indexed by an arbitrary (unspecified) index set α. The CORRECTED formalization reflects this by introducing a general type `α` and taking `A : α → Set X`. The ORIGINAL formalization restricts the index set to ℕ (`A : ℕ → Set X`), which makes it strictly weaker than the informal claim — the informal claim implies the ℕ-indexed case, but the ℕ-indexed case does not imply the general claim (it says nothing about, e.g., uncountable collections). The restricted ℕ-indexed statement is still mathematically true (provable as a special case of the general theorem, e.g., via Mathlib's connected union lemmas). The error is a misreading of the informal statement's arbitrary index set as specifically ℕ. (The duplicated `[TopologicalSpace X]` instance in the original is redundant but not semantically incorrect.)
